metadata.title: Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2

beats.narration_text: Talofa. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2
output: ✓ Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2

beats.narration_text: Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2 is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: N (terms in product). Verify: the key behaviour of wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: N (terms in produc

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2 — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from wallis-product-pi.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag N (terms in product) from 1 to 500. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of N (terms in product) from 1 to 500

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2 behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of wallis product: two diverging halves converge to π/2. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Wallis Product: Two Diverging Halves Converge to π/2 simulation and add a second slider that controls N (terms in product). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
